key_themes: Steep ~40% YTD stock decline amid SaaS/AI fears and P/E contraction
Strong Q2 execution with 17% revenue growth and AI agent adoption
Valuation reset creating major buying opportunity (avg PT ~$600–638)
AI tailwinds vs disruption risks (Intuit monetizing AI agents heavily)
Growth deceleration concerns, Mailchimp integration, and sector rotation
summary: Intuit (INTU) stock has been one of the worst-performing S&P 500 names in 2026, falling roughly 40% year-to-date and over 50% from 2025 highs due to broader SaaS sell-off, fears of general-purpose AI disrupting its tax and accounting moat, a decelerating growth outlook (Q3 guidance ~10% vs prior 17%), and Mailchimp challenges. Despite this, fundamentals remain robust: Q2 revenue rose 17% to ~$4.7B with beats, AI agents are seeing massive uptake (3M+ customers, high repeat usage, millions of transactions automated), margins are strong, and the company reiterated full-year guidance while launching buybacks and seeing insider confidence. Many investors and analysts now view the pullback as overdone, citing diversified revenue (~60% business-facing), 'Rule of 50' economics, and Intuit's ability to leverage AI for autonomous experiences rather than be replaced by it, with average price targets implying 50%+ upside from current levels near $390.
sources: 20+ recent X posts and discussions (mostly April 2026), 12+ news/analyst articles (Seeking Alpha, Yahoo Finance, Barron's, investor reports), Intuit Q2 FY2026 earnings release and commentary
